posts.text:
The issue with the current MOU status quo is that Iran believes that it secured the right to manage Hormuz and the US is acting like it didn’t.

The US is encouraging an Omani reroute around Iran (first dark, now in the open)

If Iran doesn’t challenge the reroute it loses
posts.summary: Iran faces pressure to challenge a U.S.-backed Omani shipping route that circumvents the Strait of Hormuz, risking potential disruptions to global oil transit if it escalates to protect its perceived control over the waterway. The dispute reflects underlying disagreement over Iran's authority in the region under the current agreement framework.

posts.text: CHART OF THE DAY: Brent Dated-to-Frontline spread, a key indicator of the strength (or weakness) of the physical crude market in the Atlantic. The DFL is at its weakest since mid-2023. https://t.co/IdzvmHPslv
posts.summary: The Brent Dated-to-Frontline spread, which measures physical crude market strength in the Atlantic, has weakened to its lowest level since mid-2023, suggesting reduced demand or increased supply in the physical market. This deterioration in the spread indicates softening conditions for crude oil trading.

posts.text:
Hormuz risks resurface

The QatarEnergy chartered Umm Slal has become the latest LNG tanker to reverse course near the Strait of #Hormuz, following a similar move by Gaslog Shanghai on 25 June. The reversals coincided with warnings from Iran’s IRGC that vessels must avoid https://t.co/ySMFoZdLuc
posts.summary: LNG tankers are reversing course near the Strait of Hormuz in response to Iranian IRGC warnings, signaling renewed geopolitical risks to a critical chokepoint for global energy supplies. The disruptions could potentially disrupt LNG shipments and increase shipping costs if vessels are forced to take alternate routes.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic stays elevated

Strait of Hormuz traffic remained resilient on 25 June, with 54 verified crossings across a broad mix of commercial and energy-linked vessels. West-to-East movements dominated, while the Omani Route accounted for the largest share of identified https://t.co/UOtJE35wjd
posts.summary: The Strait of Hormuz maintained robust traffic on June 25 with 54 crossings dominated by westbound-to-eastbound movements, indicating continued resilience in energy shipments through this critical chokepoint. The sustained commercial and energy vessel activity suggests stable oil transport flows despite regional tensions.

posts.text: Commodities weekly: June reset as peace hopes hawkish Fed and fund liquidation hit crowded longs https://t.co/E3LtU7TSkI via @saxobank https://t.co/86h8LSl0z7
posts.summary: June commodity prices are being pressured by peace hopes, a hawkish Federal Reserve stance, and fund liquidation of crowded long positions. The oil market is likely experiencing downward pressure as a result of these combined factors.

posts.text: Power outage slows operations of key port, plants after earthquakes in Venezuela https://t.co/rCVLtOy7t4
posts.summary: Power outages triggered by earthquakes in Venezuela are disrupting operations at critical port and industrial facilities, potentially impacting the country's oil production and export capacity. This could have supply implications for global crude markets given Venezuela's role as a major oil producer.

posts.text:
A huge surplus in global oil markets going into the Hormuz crisis provided a major cushion against the initial shock

But with global oil inventories falling at a record pace since then, the recovery in flows through the Strait has come at a critical time: https://t.co/vvjkiIlFD9 https://t.co/xKRDhCpMjb
posts.summary: Global oil inventories have fallen at a record pace since the Hormuz crisis began, making the recent recovery in oil flows through the Strait critically important to prevent further supply tightness, though an initial market surplus had provided some initial protection from the disruption shock.

posts.text: UK government to subsidise bank loans for green home improvements https://t.co/59huv9roVO
posts.summary: The UK government's subsidy program for bank loans supporting green home improvements may reduce residential energy demand from fossil fuels, potentially creating headwinds for oil consumption in the heating and building sectors.

posts.text:
Demand flexibility is already helping manage periods of peak stress in power systems

And as electrification advances, it could evolve into an everyday tool for boosting system resilience & efficiency

Our new report looks at how to unlock these benefits: https://t.co/PkdA1zcLLV https://t.co/BmMyLV7gda
posts.summary: The IEA highlights demand flexibility as an emerging tool for managing power system stress and improving resilience as electrification expands, which could reduce strain on energy infrastructure and influence how electricity demand is managed in the future.

posts.text: Canada and Japan are deepening cooperation on critical minerals as both countries seek to reduce reliance on China’s supply chain dominance. The partnership could include joint stockpiling, new mining projects, and expanded energy exports. #CriticalMinerals #Canada #Japan
posts.summary: Canada and Japan are collaborating to diversify critical minerals sourcing away from China, which could strengthen Canada's position as an alternative energy and resource supplier. The partnership's focus on joint stockpiling and expanded energy exports may support higher demand for Canadian oil and mineral commodities.

posts.text: Big Oil’s green pivot is losing momentum. Faced with investor pressure, weaker renewable returns, and stronger oil & gas profits, major energy companies are shifting capital back to hydrocarbons while taking a more selective approach to low-carbon investments. #Oil
posts.summary: Major energy companies are redirecting investment capital from renewable energy back toward oil and gas production due to investor pressure and more attractive hydrocarbon returns. This shift signals a slowdown in the industry's transition away from fossil fuels.

posts.text: Germany urges EU to suspend methane rules after US pressure https://t.co/r6JrmxXoYT
posts.summary: Germany is reportedly pushing the EU to suspend methane regulations amid pressure from the US, a development that could potentially ease environmental compliance costs for oil and gas producers. The move may have mixed implications for crude markets, depending on whether it affects upstream production incentives or downstream refining operations across Europe.

posts.text: After a one day respite, the June Swoon for crude and gasoline prices has resumed. Retail gasoline prices barely top $3.90/gal and look to target $3.75-$3.80/gal averages for July 4th weekend. Diesel plunge likely to accelerate as well.
posts.summary: Crude oil and gasoline prices are declining again after a brief pause, with retail gasoline expected to average between $3.75-$3.80 per gallon by the July 4th weekend and diesel prices anticipated to fall further. The "June Swoon" trend indicates continued downward pressure on energy prices heading into the summer holiday period.

posts.text: Russia may be about to import gasoline cargoes from India, thanks to its damaged refining infrastructure. They are probably short about 125,000 b/d of motor fuel and India typically exports about four times that amount. Ukrainian attacks on Russia are creating strange alliances.
posts.summary: Russia may need to import approximately 125,000 barrels per day of gasoline from India due to Ukrainian attacks damaging its refining capacity, while India has sufficient export capacity to meet this demand. This development highlights how geopolitical conflicts are reshaping global oil trade patterns and supply chains.

UKMTO has received a report of an incident within the Strait of Hormuz.

The Master of Tanker has reported being struck by an unidentified projectile. The vessel sustained damage to their bridge; all crew are reported safe. No environmental damage reported at present. https://t.co/vftCd84D2z
posts.summary: A tanker was struck by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, sustaining bridge damage while crew remained safe, raising concerns about maritime security in a critical global oil transit chokepoint. The incident could heighten shipping risks and potentially impact oil market volatility given the strait's importance to global energy supplies.
